title: Challenge: AWS_VPC_NACL_Lab - Case Study on VPC, NACL Configuration, and Compliance Auditing

tasks.title: Sign into the AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click Open Console to access the AWS Console in a new tab.
On the AWS sign-in page, leave the Account ID as default, copy your Username and Password from the Lab Console, and sign in.
Set the AWS Region to US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.
Sign into the AWS Management Console

XYZ Company wants to set up a secure network infrastructure on AWS using a Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) with public and private subnets, control traffic using Network Access Control Lists (NACLs), and monitor compliance using AWS Config. Your job is to create a VPC, configure subnets, set up an internet gateway, launch EC2 instances, configure a custom NACL, audit the setup for compliance, and validate the setup. Here’s how to proceed:
Create Subnets (Public, Private)
Create and Attach an Internet Gateway
Create Route Tables and Associate Them with Subnets (Public, Private)
Update Route Table and Configure the Internet Gateway
Enable Auto-Assign Public IP for Public Subnets
Launch an EC2 Instance in the Public Subnet AMI: Amazon Linux 2023 kernel AMI. Instance type: t2.micro. Network settings: Subnet: Select PublicSubnet. Auto-assign public IP:
AMI: Amazon Linux 2023 kernel AMI.
Instance type: t2.micro.
Network settings: Subnet: Select PublicSubnet. Auto-assign public IP:
Subnet: Select PublicSubnet.
Auto-assign public IP:
Create security group with port 20 and port 80
User script: install http and some text in index.html
AMI: Amazon Linux 2023 kernel AMI.
Instance type: t2.micro.
Network settings: Subnet: Select PrivateSubnet.
Subnet: Select PrivateSubnet.
Auto-assign public IP:
Create a new security group with port 22
Test the Public EC2 Instance: Paste Ip in browser check for webpage
Paste Ip in browser check for webpage
Test the Private EC2 Instance: Connect by bastion host Ping from private Ip
Connect by bastion host
Ping from private Ip
Testing the Public and Private Server
Test the Public EC2 Instance: You will get site can’t be reached
You will get site can’t be reached
Test the Private EC2 Instance: You can't access as public instance in is not reach
You can't access as public instance in is not reach
Add the following inbound rules: Rule 100: SSH (port 22). Rule 200: HTTP (port 80) Rule 150: All ICMP IPV4.
Rule 100: SSH (port 22).
Rule 200: HTTP (port 80)
Rule 150: All ICMP IPV4.
Edit outbound rules.
Add the following rules: Rule 100: Custom TCP (1024 - 65535) Allow. Rule 150: All ICMP IPV4, Allow. Rule 200: SSH (20), Allow.
Rule 100: Custom TCP (1024 - 65535) Allow.
Rule 150: All ICMP IPV4, Allow.
Rule 200: SSH (20), Allow.
Try to ping private EC2 from public EC2
Confirm the VPC is created with the correct CIDR block.
Verify the public and private subnets are created and associated with the correct route tables.
Ensure the internet gateway is attached and configured in PublicRouteTable.
Confirm PublicEC2 is launched in PublicSubnet with a public IP and can access the internet.
Confirm PrivateEC2 is launched in PrivateSubnet without a public IP and cannot access the internet.
Verify the custom NACL is created, associated with PublicSubnet, and has the correct rules.
Confirm the connectivity tests align with the NACL rules and subnet configurations.
Click Validate in the lab console to check the setup.
Sign out of AWS by clicking your username (top-right) and selecting Sign out.
Click End Lab in the lab console to complete the lab.
